title: Resume Normal Navigation
disclaimer: Training support only. Confirm phraseology and local route recovery procedures with an instructor.
learning_objectives: Explain what Resume Normal Navigation means after a temporary deviation.
Use Resume Nav to return an aircraft from heading or radial behavior toward its planned route.
Confirm that the aircraft returns to the destination route, not the last temporary instruction.
key_points: Resume Normal Navigation cancels the temporary tactical instruction.
The aircraft should return toward its planned route from its current position.
It should not jump back to the old position or resume the last temporary radial.

lesson_steps.title: Cancel The Temporary Instruction
lesson_steps.body: Resume Normal Navigation tells the aircraft to stop following the temporary heading, radial, direct, or holding behavior and return to its route.

lesson_steps.title: Current Position Matters
lesson_steps.body: The aircraft should recover from where it is now. It should not reset to an earlier point.

lesson_steps.title: Recover From Deviation
script: Assign a heading or radial deviation.
Wait until the aircraft is visibly away from the route.
Use Resume Nav and monitor route recovery.

exercise.title: Return One Deviated Aircraft To Route
exercise.objective: Recover an aircraft from a temporary deviation without resetting its position.
student_actions: Wait long enough to see displacement.
Monitor the aircraft returning toward its route.
success_conditions: The aircraft cancels the temporary instruction.
The aircraft recovers from its current position.
The aircraft resumes the planned route rather than the last temporary radial.
debrief_prompts: Did the aircraft recover from its current position?
How far did the aircraft deviate before recovery?
